versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: return to Me,” declares the LORD.
“If you will remove your detestable idols from My sight
2 and if you can swear, ‘As surely as the LORD lives,’
in truth, in justice, and in righteousness,
then the nations will be blessed by Him,
and in Him they will glory.”
3 For this is what the LORD says to the men of Judah and Jerusalem:
and do not sow among the thorns.
and remove the foreskins of your hearts,
O men of Judah and people of Jerusalem.
Otherwise, My wrath will break out like fire
and burn with no one to extinguish it,

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 4**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **A Plea to Return** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 4** remains the first control on meaning.
